findings.claim_text: Its headline finding is a hedge, not a declaration: "We are not there yet, and recursive self-improvement is not inevitable."

Anthropic's flagship science product, Claude Science, launched eleven days later on June 30, 2026; MIT Technology Review's and STAT News's dedicated launch coverage credit Alexander Tarashansky and Eric Kauderer-Abrams as the people who built and lead it — neither mentions Jumper.

On the one clean, apples-to-apples measure we found — academic poaching in H1 2026, where at least 22 professors left universities for frontier labs — Anthropic (5) trails OpenAI and Meta (6 each) and ties Google DeepMind (5).
